name: Zhao Wei

sections.biography-source-undefined:
Zhao Wei was from Baxi commandery. He left the imperial government to venture with Liu Yan into Yi province when the latter was appointed as its governor. He became a prominent official under Liu Yan, and when Liu Yan died in 194, it was Zhao Wei who orchestrated the succession of Liu Zhang because he believed that Liu Zhang was too gentle and would be politically weak as a governor. Liu Zhang entrusted a lot of authority to Zhao Wei.

Zhao Wei then went on an expedition against Liu Biao in Jing province (it is also said Liu Zhang ordered this), but hearing that Liu Zhang was unpopular among his people, began to plot rebellion, and soon made peace with Liu Biao after giving him generous gifts. He was successful in connecting with local officials and began his rebellion, gaining Shu, Guanghan, and Jianwei commanderies. Unfortunately, he was hated by the Army of the Eastern Provinces that were under Liu Zhang, and they continuously dealt his rebel army heavy blows for Liu Zhang. Zhao Wei fell back to Jiangzhou and was pursued by the provincial armies. Before there was any fighting, Pang Le (龐樂) and Li Yi (李異), who were under Zhao Wei, beheaded their superior and destroyed parts of the rebel army, bringing an end to his rebellion.

full_text:
Zhao Wei 趙韙
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Zhao Wei (趙韙)was born in Ba. Rebelled against Liu Zhang but killed by own men. Zhao Wei was affiliated with the Han Dynasty, Liu Yan, and Liu Zhang. Zhao Wei lived during the Later Han Dynasty periods of Chinese history.

BiographySource Undefined
Zhao Wei was from Baxi commandery. He left the imperial government to venture with Liu Yan into Yi province when the latter was appointed as its governor. He became a prominent official under Liu Yan, and when Liu Yan died in 194, it was Zhao Wei who orchestrated the succession of Liu Zhang because he believed that Liu Zhang was too gentle and would be politically weak as a governor. Liu Zhang entrusted a lot of authority to Zhao Wei.
Zhao Wei then went on an expedition against Liu Biao in Jing province (it is also said Liu Zhang ordered this), but hearing that Liu Zhang was unpopular among his people, began to plot rebellion, and soon made peace with Liu Biao after giving him generous gifts. He was successful in connecting with local officials and began his rebellion, gaining Shu, Guanghan, and Jianwei commanderies. Unfortunately, he was hated by the Army of the Eastern Provinces that were under Liu Zhang, and they continuously dealt his rebel army heavy blows for Liu Zhang. Zhao Wei fell back to Jiangzhou and was pursued by the provincial armies. Before there was any fighting, Pang Le (龐樂) and Li Yi (李異), who were under Zhao Wei, beheaded their superior and destroyed parts of the rebel army, bringing an end to his rebellion.
